Source_Species.reason: The paper studies OsAMT1;1 in rice.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Rice is the study species; OsAMT1;1 indicates Oryza sativa.

Applied_Species.reason: Transgenic and mutant analyses were performed in rice.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The applied species throughout is rice, i.e. Oryza sativa.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Methods state pAMT1;1:Amtrac high capacity was transformed into Japonica rice cultivar Dongjin (DJ); figures also use DJ for AMT1;1 OX/RNAi comparisons.

Applied_Promoters.reason: The paper explicitly states AMT1;1 endogenous promoter was used for the Amtrac construct; it does not clearly specify the promoter for AMT1;1 OX, matching the field wording.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Results directly link AMT1;1 function to altered amino acid levels (Glu/Gln) and chlorophyll accumulation.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Amino acids and chlorophyll are directly discussed; chlorophyll is a tetrapyrrole pigment.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Amino acids were measured in leaves, and chlorophyll content was measured in leaves.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Results show higher amino acids/chlorophyll associate with increased resistance to sheath blight; photosynthetic capacity is not directly tested but chlorophyll function is broadly consistent. Main defense link is supported.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show AMT1;1 promotes NH4+ uptake, affects assimilation-related outputs, increases resistance under LN/MN but not HN, and some yield traits under LN are improved.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: BiFC and split-ubiquitin assays show AMT1;1 D358N interacts with AMT1;1, AMT1;2 and AMT1;3.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: qRT-PCR used leaves, and functional effects are shown under low- and medium-N conditions.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: AMT proteins are membrane transporters, but this paper does not explicitly report plasma membrane localization for AMT1;1.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are present in Results for AMT1;1-related claims.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: AMT1;1 is explicitly an ammonium transporter and part of plant AMTs; transmembrane nature is shown/discussed.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Results include R. solani AG1-IA infection, low/medium/high N fertilization, and NH4+ treatment.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Results show resistance advantage disappears under high N; no major yield penalty is reported.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Both points are directly supported: D358N acts dominant-negatively on AMT1;1/1;2/1;3, and gs1;1 epistasis supports assimilation rather than NH4+ accumulation itself.

Omics_Data.reason: The paper explicitly references previous transcriptome data and no accession is provided here.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Discussion concludes AMT1;1 OX can improve NUE and ShB resistance especially under limited N; this application value is directly inferred from results/discussion.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summary points match the Results: D358N reduces NH4+ uptake and increases susceptibility, AMT1;1 OX enhances resistance while broader AMT1 suppression reduces it, and defense is tied to assimilation/chlorophyll/amino acids/ethylene signaling under low N.
